params.name: The Works of Nature
params.description:
On May 5, 1725 AD, Chen Menglei included the entire book in the Imperial Encyclopaedia.
The Works of Nature, compiled by the Ming dynasty scientist Song Yingxing, is an 18-chapter book documenting agricultural and handicraft technologies such as mechanisms, bricks, ceramics, salt production, and gunpowder. It is a testament to the achievements of ancient Chinese industrial technologies and is regarded by scholars as the Chinese technical encyclopedia of the 17th century.
